creator: lawrance@FreeBSD.org
text:
Martin Kammerhofer wrote:
> Zitat von Sam Lawrance <lawrance@FreeBSD.org>:
>
>> Synopsis: port devel/ac-archive installs empty tarball
>>
>> This port is unmaintained.  Can you help us out by providing a patch to
>> fix the problem, or at least a good description of what changes you
>> think need to be made?  Thanks!
>
> Hello Sam!
>
> I asked Peter Simons <simons@cryp.to> (the maintainer of 
> http://autoconf-archive.cryp.to) about the relation to 
> ac-archive.sf.net and got this reply:
>
>> Hey Martin,
>>
>> the site ac-archive.sf.net is maintained by Guido Draheim. He is
>> essentially re-releasing the contents of my site under his own
>> name without giving me credit. It's a bit of a sad story, so I
>> won't go into that in detail. Every now and then, he makes a few
>> minor changes of his own and publishes a new release to make it
>> appear that his site would be more up-to-date than
>> autoconf-archive.cryp.to.
>
> Provided that the sf.net redistribution is broken since Dec. 2005 I 
> suggest
> switching to cryp.to.
> I can prepare and submit a patch till next week (maybe earlier) and
> think of doing it like this:
> 1. Create new port devel/autoconf-archive with new upstream source.
> 2A. Mark old devel/ac-archive as long time BROKEN and refer to new port
>  -OR-
> 2B. Remove long time broken devel/ac-archive and add the
>     ac-archive => autoconf-archive transition to MOVED.
>

2B is the best option, I think.  We'll have to wait for a week or two 
before committing the changes, because ports are frozen in preparation 
for 6.2-RELEASE right now.

Cheres
Sam
